Task: [Chat Module - Supplier Dashboard] Implement Dynamic Text Truncation for Chat Messages
Description: Add logic to handle and display long text messages within the chat interface. The system should differentiate between continuous long text (without spaces) and normal sentences (with spaces), applying specific truncation rules for each case to ensure clean display and prevent layout breaks. Acceptance Criteria: Continuous Text Handling (No Spaces): If the input text is a single continuous string with no spaces (e.g., “hellooooooooooooooo”), display only the first 10 characters.Append "..." at the end of the truncated text.Example: Input: helloooooooooooooooOutput: helloooooo... [*] Sentence Handling (With Spaces): If the text contains spaces, display only the first 2 words followed by "...".Example: Input: Hello there, how are you?Output: Hello there...

Task: User Story: Ex-Works (EXW) Checkout
Description: Title: Checkout with Ex-Works Shipping Option As a customer I want to select Ex-Works (EXW) as the shipping method during checkout So that I can arrange pickup and shipping of the goods directly from the seller’s location. Description Ex-Works (EXW) allows the buyer to take full responsibility for shipping, customs clearance, and delivery from the seller’s warehouse or store location. During checkout, the user can select EXW instead of standard shipping options. Acceptance Criteria Shipping Method Visibility EXW option is displayed in the shipping methods section if the product supports EXW.The option clearly states: “Buyer arranges pickup from seller location.” Seller Location Display When EXW is selected, the seller’s pickup address and contact details are shown. Shipping Cost Shipping cost is set to 0 when EXW is selected.Order summary updates dynamically after selection. Pickup Acknowledgment User must check a confirmation box:"“I understand that I am responsible for pickup, shipping, and customs clearance.” " Order Confirmation Order status is marked as: Pending Pickup after successful payment. Seller receives notification that order is EXW. Notifications Buyer receives confirmation email including: Seller pickup addressContact informationOrder reference number Admin Panel Admin can see shipping type = EXW in order details.Admin can filter orders by shipping method (EXW).
Observers: Hesham Raafat, Mohamed Salah, Anwar Abdelmaksoud, Muhammed Talaat, Yara Mahmoud

Task: Seller Pickup Information Section
Description: Display seller pickup details when EXW selected: Seller NamePickup AddressCity / CountryContact Phone Hide this section when non-EXW method selected.UI styling to differentiate pickup info box.

Description: Add required checkbox:"“I understand that I am responsible for pickup, shipping, and customs clearance.” "Disable “Place Order” button until checkbox is checked.Show validation error if user tries to submit without confirming.

Task: Order Confirmation Page Update
Description: If shipping method = EXW: Display label: “Shipping Method: Ex-Works (EXW)”Display seller pickup address again.Show status badge: Pending Pickup Add informational message about pickup process.

Task: [Admin-1] User Story: Settings Management
Description: As an Admin I want to manage platform settings from the Settings page So that I can control system configurations from one place. Description The Settings screen contains multiple setting cards/sections where the Admin can: Update general platform informationConfigure system optionsToggle activation statusEdit configurable valuesSave changes per section Each settings block appears as a separate card with editable fields and a Save button. ✅ Acceptance Criteria 1️⃣ Access Control Only Admin users can access the Settings page.Unauthorized users cannot view the page. 2️⃣ Editable Settings Sections Settings are displayed in grouped cards.Each card contains: Input fields (text / number).Toggle buttons (Enable / Disable).Status indicators (Active / Inactive badges).Save button for that section. 3️⃣ Field Behavior Input fields: Support inline validation.Show error messages for invalid values. Toggle buttons: Change status visually (Active / Inactive).Update value on save. Disabled settings appear visually different from active ones. 4️⃣ Save Action Clicking Save: Validates all fields in the section.Shows loading state.Displays success message on success.Displays error message if request fails. Changes persist after page refresh.
Observers: Amr Mohamed, Muhammed Talaat, Yara Mahmoud

Task: Settings Repository / Service Layer
Description: Create SettingsService class.Methods: getAllGrouped()getByKey($key)update($key, $value)updateGroup($group, $payload)toggleStatus($key) Implement caching layer (Redis / file cache).Clear cache automatically on update.

Task: API Endpoints "Get settings - update settings"
Description: Get Settings GET /admin/settings Return grouped settings.Only accessible by Admin role. 🔹 Update Settings (Per Section) PUT /admin/settings/{group} Validate payload.Update multiple keys inside that group.Return updated data. 🔹 Toggle Setting Status PATCH /admin/settings/{key}/toggle Switch is_active state.Return updated status.

Task: [Admin-2] user story Admin Control over Merchant and Supplier Access
Description: As an admin, I want to manage the status of merchants and suppliers (suspend or block), So that suspended users can login but have restricted actions, and blocked users are prevented from logging in or causing issues with ongoing orders. Acceptance Criteria: When a merchant is suspended, they can login but cannot add products to cart or proceed to checkout.When a supplier is suspended, they can login but their products are hidden from the website.When a merchant is blocked, they cannot login.When a supplier is blocked, if they have ongoing orders, they cannot be blocked until the orders are completed.
Observers: Amr Mohamed, Hesham Raafat, Muhammed Talaat, Yara Mahmoud

Task: [Admin-4] User Story — Membership Management
Description: Title: Manage Membership for Suppliers As a Super Admin I want to manage the Membership configuration and assign it to suppliers So that all suppliers have a membership level that controls their participation in the platform. Description The system will contain a Membership module where the Super Admin can manage membership details. The system will initially contain one default membership created through a seed. All newly registered suppliers will automatically receive the default membership. The Super Admin can later update the membership details or change the membership assigned to a supplier. Acceptance Criteria The system must contain one default membership created through a seed during system setup.The Membership module must include the following fields: Name (Arabic)Name (English)PhotoStatus (Active / Deactive) The Super Admin can: View membership detailsAdd membership (if none exists)Edit membershipDelete membership The system must not allow more than one membership record at the same time.When a new supplier is created or registered, the system automatically assigns the default membership.The Super Admin can change the membership assigned to a supplier from the Admin Dashboard.Membership can be activated or deactivated by the Super Admin.Only users with the Super Admin role can manage the Membership module.

[Userstory] FCA Trade Term Order Handling
Description: Title: Handle FCA Shipping for Multiple Warehousing Locations As a Merchant I want to choose the warehousing location and the quantity of products delivered to each location when ordering products with the FCA trade term So that the supplier can deliver the products to the correct warehouse locations according to the merchant’s shipping plan. Description When a merchant creates an order containing products with the FCA (Free Carrier) trade term, the merchant is responsible for selecting the delivery warehouse location where the supplier will hand over the goods. If the order contains multiple quantities of a product, the merchant can distribute the quantities across different warehouse addresses. For example: If the merchant orders 3 products, the merchant can choose which warehouse(s) will receive the shipment and specify the quantity delivered to each warehouse. Scenario Merchant adds 3 products with FCA trade term to the cart.During checkout, the Shipping Details section appears.The system displays the merchant’s saved warehouse addresses.The merchant selects one or more warehouses.The merchant enters the quantity of products to ship to each warehouse.The total quantity distributed across warehouses must equal the total ordered quantity. Acceptance Criteria 1. FCA Shipping Section When the product trade term is FCA, the Shipping Details section appears in checkout. 2. Warehouse Selection The merchant can: View available warehouse addresses.Select one or multiple warehouses for delivery. Each warehouse card includes: Warehouse nameAddressQuantity input field 3. Quantity Distribution The merchant must enter: Quantity to ship to each warehouse Rules: Quantity must be greater than 0Total distributed quantity must equal the ordered quantity Example: [th]Warehouse [/th][th]Quantity [/th]Riyadh Dry Port 1 Jeddah Port 2 Total = 3 product

Task: Add Product button disabled when Trade Term is FOB and RFQ is selected
Description: Description: When adding a product, if the Trade Term is set to FOB and the user selects RFQ, the Add Product button becomes disabled. This prevents the user from submitting the product, even though all required fields are filled correctly. Expected Result: The Add Product button should remain enabled and allow the product to be submitted. Actual Result: The Add Product button is disabled, preventing the product submission.

Task: RFQ field in Add Product accepts only integers and does not allow decimal values
Description: Description: In the Add Product section, when selecting RFQ, the related field only accepts integer values and does not allow decimal (float) values. This restricts users from entering accurate values when decimals are required. Expected Result: The field should accept decimal (float) values if applicable. Actual Result: The field only accepts integer values and rejects decimal inputs.

Task: RFQ field in Add Product accepts only integers and does not allow decimal values
Description: Description: In the Add Product section, when selecting RFQ, the related field only accepts integer values and does not allow decimal (float) values. This restricts users from entering accurate values when decimals are required. Expected Result: The field should accept decimal (float) values if applicable. Actual Result: The field only accepts integer values and rejects decimal inputs.

Task: Port and Lead Time fields are not required when creating RFQ Request
Description: Description: When creating an RFQ Request, the Port and Lead Time fields are not marked as required. This allows the user to submit the request without filling these important fields, which leads to incomplete data. Expected Result: The Port and Lead Time fields should be required, and the system should prevent submission if they are empty. Actual Result: The system allows submission of the RFQ Request without filling Port and Lead Time.

Task: Need to enhancement this screen
Incorrect text direction when switching to Arabic language
Description: Description: When changing the website language to Arabic, the text is translated correctly, but the text direction remains incorrect (LTR instead of RTL). This affects the readability and overall UI alignment. Expected Result: The website should display content in Right-to-Left (RTL) direction when Arabic is selected. Actual Result: The text appears in Arabic, but the direction remains Left-to-Right (LTR).

Task: Backend response messages are not localized to Arabic
Description: Description: After receiving responses from the backend, the system displays messages only in English without providing an Arabic translation. This creates an inconsistent user experience when the application language is set to Arabic. Expected Result: All messages should be displayed in Arabic when the application language is set to Arabic. Actual Result: Messages are displayed only in English and are not translated into Arabic.
